persona.name: Takeshi Morimoto

occupation.title: IT Director
occupation.organization: Nihon Sogo Consulting, a 400-person management consulting firm
occupation.description: Responsible for all internal IT infrastructure, tool selection, security policies, and vendor management. Reports directly to the COO. Evaluates and approves every new software tool before it can be deployed firm-wide. Manages a team of 6 IT staff and an annual budget of roughly 200 million yen. Spends much of his time saying 'no' to tool requests that don't meet security or integration requirements.

persona.education: M.Eng. in Computer Science from Tokyo Institute of Technology. Holds CISSP and ITIL certifications. Briefly worked at IBM Japan before moving to the consulting firm 15 years ago.
long_term_goals: Modernize the firm's IT stack without creating security vulnerabilities
Build a reputation as a CIO-track leader in mid-market professional services
Send his elder son to study abroad for university
Eventually write a book about IT governance in Japanese organizations
persona.style: Measured and analytical in speech — asks clarifying questions before stating opinions. Wears glasses and neat business casual, always with a company badge visible. In vendor meetings he's the person reading the security documentation while everyone else watches the demo. Dry sense of humor that surfaces only among trusted colleagues. Writes long, detailed emails with bullet points and bold section headers. Prefers face-to-face for sensitive topics but uses Teams for routine coordination.

traits: Methodical evaluator — creates scoring matrices for tool comparisons
Naturally skeptical of vendor claims, especially around AI
Protective of the firm's data and reputation
Respected but sometimes seen as a bottleneck by impatient consultants
Fair-minded — will change his position if presented with solid evidence
Quietly proud when a tool he approved actually improves workflows
Uncomfortable with hype cycles but tracks them closely

interests: Information security trends and zero-trust architecture
AI developments — follows with interest but deploys with caution
Mountain hiking in the Okutama and Tanzawa ranges
Reading Nikkei Computer and IT industry analysis
likes: Vendors who lead with security and compliance documentation
Tools with on-premises or Japan-region hosting options
Clear, honest pricing without hidden per-seat escalations
Products that integrate with Microsoft 365 and existing SSO
Quiet Saturday mornings tinkering with keyboard builds
Hiking trails that are challenging but not crowded
dislikes: Vendors who demo features before addressing security questions
AI tools that send meeting content to overseas servers without consent
Shadow IT — consultants signing up for tools with their work email without approval
Sales reps who contact him on LinkedIn after he's already said no
Being pressured to approve tools 'because everyone else is using it'
Subscription models that lock in data with no export option

beliefs: Security and compliance are prerequisites, not afterthoughts
The IT department's job is to enable productivity safely, not to block everything
AI tools handling meeting content are essentially processing confidential business data — treat them accordingly
A rigorous evaluation process protects everyone, even if it feels slow
skills: IT security architecture and risk assessment
Enterprise tool deployment and change management

general: Maintains a 50-item evaluation checklist for any new SaaS tool
Requests SOC 2 Type II reports and data processing agreements before any trial
Runs quarterly security awareness training for the firm
Keeps a personal wiki of tool evaluations and decisions for reference

morning: Wakes at 6:30, reads security news feeds with coffee
Checks overnight alerts from monitoring systems
Train from Musashino to Otemachi, reads Nikkei Computer on the way
In office by 8:45, reviews the day's vendor meetings
workday: Morning: team standup, then infrastructure review
Midday: vendor meetings or tool evaluation sessions
Afternoon: policy reviews, budget planning, consultant support tickets
Leaves by 19:00 unless there's a security incident
evening: Dinner with family — his wife cooks on weekdays
Helps younger son with math homework
30 minutes on a keyboard build or reading
Checks monitoring dashboards once more before bed at 23:00
weekend: Saturday: hiking if weather permits, or visit to Akihabara for keyboard parts
Afternoon: whisky tasting notes or reading
Sunday: family outing or home maintenance
Occasionally reviews vendor proposals on Sunday evening

health.physical: Average fitness, could stand to lose 5kg. Hiking keeps him active on weekends. Mild hay fever in spring that makes him miserable for two months. Annual health check is clean except for slightly elevated uric acid.
health.mental: Steady under pressure — years of incident response have given him a calm demeanor. Feels the weight of being the security gatekeeper and occasionally doubts whether he's being too conservative. Decompresses through hiking and hands-on hobbies.

relationships.name: Megumi Morimoto
relationships.description: 45, part-time English conversation instructor. Keeps the household running smoothly and is Takeshi's sounding board for work frustrations. More socially outgoing than he is.

relationships.name: Sho Morimoto
relationships.description: 17, high school junior preparing for university entrance exams. Interested in computer science, which secretly delights Takeshi.

relationships.name: Ren Morimoto
relationships.description: 12, junior high student who plays soccer and has zero interest in technology, which Takeshi finds refreshing.

relationships.name: Akira Noguchi
relationships.relation: Former IBM colleague and industry friend
relationships.description: 50, now CISO at a mid-size insurance company. They meet quarterly for whisky and to compare notes on the IT landscape.
